title: 1981-04-16_Pat_105.txt
key_claims: Dick explicitly identifies Ronald Reagan as the new "Caesar" of the Empire. The apocalyptic cypher of Rome and the blood games now applies to the modern destruction of animal life and the environment.

The Kerygma of the Third Dispensation is that there is no more middle ground: humanity is faced with an absolute, Zoroastrian choice between total spiritual life (saving the ecosphere) and absolute physical death (doom).
In the past, rejecting the spiritual simply meant living a mundane life. Now, because Christ *is* the ecosphere, rejecting the spiritual (by poisoning it) guarantees physical planetary extinction.
He realizes this message is killing him physically, but he cannot stop promulgating it. He contrasts this vast spiritual burden with the comfortable life he could be living.
recurring_concepts: **The Third Dispensation**: The revolutionary idea that spirituality and physical ecology are now perfectly homologized. Saving the planet *is* saving Christ.
reading_excerpt: Dick explicitly identifies Ronald Reagan as the new "Caesar" of the Empire. The apocalyptic cypher of Rome and the blood games now applies to the modern destruction of animal life and the environment.
